title: Immunoblotting protocol  for dopamine and cortical iPSC-derived neurons with different Parkinsonian mutations
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5smi3","text":"This protocol details the method for immunoblotting of differentiated VMDA and cortical iPSC's from different Parkinson's mutations for quantification of Parkin, α-syn, Rab-10 and glucocerebrosidase proteins.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"tg9d","text":"Ventral midbrain progenitors and Cortical neural progenitors were generated from iPSC according to the methods of Gantner and colleagues (1, 2).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Thaw samples and centrifuge at 12000 rpm, 4 °C, , 4°C, 00:20:00
Thaw samples from freezer on ice and place on heating block for 00:10:00 at 70 °C
Obtain large container and fill with 1 X transfer buffer
Prepare primary antibody in 5% skim milk in TBS-T solution (dilution 1:1000)
Visualise bands using BioRad ChemiDoc MP Imaging System chemiluminescence mode
If stripping membrane is required to reprobe withanother antibody, add Abcam mild stripping buffer incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature with rocking.
Store samples at -20 °C until ready to run western blot
Prepare 30 µL sample for loading by adding 20 ug of protein lysate to required volme of dH2O and 7.5 µL of 4X NuPage LDS sample Buffer
Perform BCA assay as per manufacturer's protocol to determine protein concentration of lysates
Transfer supernatant to new microcentrifuge tubes and discard pellet.
Run gel at 180 V (constant voltage) for 01:00:00 at Room temperature
Load 4 µL of the ladder and 25 µL of the samples prepared at 1 mg/mL (or as needed)
After samples have heated, quickly vortex and spin down.
Place 12-well gel(s) into casket and clip in place. Fill up the centre part with 1 X MES buffer and check for any leaks. Once in place, add 1 X MES to the outer space until half way full.
While waiting for samples to heat up remove the white sticker and gel comb carefully from gel(s) and rinse with dH2O.
Place membrane in container and cover with 5% skim milk in TBS-T solution, place on rocking platform for 01:00:00
Perform transfer at 90 V (constant voltage) for 01:30:00 in cold room
Open sandwich apparatus and place components in the following order: sponge, filter paper, gel, nitrocellulose membrane, filter paper, sponge
For each gel obtain a sandwich apparatus, 2 X sponges, 2 X filter paper, 1 X nitrocellulose membrane (0.45 um)
Wash blots with TBS-T every 3 times for 00:10:00
Incubation at Room temperature for 01:00:00 with gentle rocking
Prepare secondary antibody (anti-mouse or anti-rabbit HRP conjugated antibody) in 2.5% skim milk in TBS-T solution
Wash blots with TBS-T every 3 times for 00:10:00
Incubation at 4 °C overnight with gentle rocking
Cut blots using scissors into smaller strips as desired
Band analysis and quantication is performed in ImageLab, all proteins are normalised to B- actin used as a loading control
Repeat step 17 onwards with new primary antibody as required.
Wash membrane 3 times with TBS-T for 00:10:00 each, them block in 5% skim milk in TBS-T solution for 01:00:00
creator: courtney.wright Wright
